functionDescription.value: Sucralose is a high-intensity artificial sweetener, roughly several hundred times sweeter than table sugar. It is used to give products a sweet taste while contributing little or no energy, so manufacturers reach for it when reformulating to cut sugar, for example in reduced-sugar soft drinks.
functionDescription.note: Descriptive scaffold record (plan 03a-05). The plain-English function is transcribed from a single tertiary registry entry pending the proof set. The corroborating passes that would publish this fact are authored by the human checkpoint in plan 03a-06, so it correctly renders withheld here (D-11/D-13).

regulatoryStatus.value: Sucralose (E955) is an authorised sweetener for use in Great Britain, permitted in defined food categories under assimilated food additives law.
regulatoryStatus.note: Descriptive scaffold record (plan 03a-05). The GB regulatory position is a placeholder citing the Food Standards Agency as the GB authority; the specific authoritative additives-law citation and the verification pass that would publish this fact are authored by the human checkpoint in plan 03a-06, so it correctly renders withheld here (D-11/D-13). It is deliberately not fabricated as verified.
